article_title: Meta Delays Avocado AI Model to May After Internal Testing Reveals Performance Gaps with Google, OpenAI and Anthropic
summary: Round 2 re-review. All three issues from Round 1 have been properly addressed. Fabricated Jensen Huang/Nvidia claim removed and replaced with verifiable structural observation. NYT URL fixed with www. prefix. Megan Fu attribution properly anonymized. All factual claims verified against multiple sources.

content_preview.title: Meta Delays Avocado AI Model to May After Internal Testing Reveals Performance Gaps with Google, OpenAI and Anthropic
content_preview.summary: Meta postpones its next-generation Avocado AI model by at least two months after benchmarks show it trailing rivals, raising questions about the company's $135 billion AI investment.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Meta has pushed the release of its next-generation AI model, codenamed Avocado, from March to at least May 2026 after internal testing revealed it trails the latest systems from Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic in key capability areas, according to [The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/03/12/technology/meta-avocado-ai-model-delay.html). The delay puts fresh scrutiny on the company's plan to spend between $115 billion and $135 billion on AI infrastructure this year.

## What...

recommendations: Consider adding money.usnews.com to config/source_allowlist.txt for future submissions
NYT URL returns 404 to automated checks but article confirmed to exist via web search and third-party citations

editor_notes.source_verification: Round 2 verification. All 3 sources re-verified. (1) NYT (www.nytimes.com/2026/03/12/technology/meta-avocado-ai-model-delay.html): returns 404 to bots but confirmed to exist via web search — cited by Fortune, PYMNTS, American Bazaar, Yahoo Finance, and dozens of outlets. URL now correctly includes www. prefix (Round 1 fix). (2) Fortune (fortune.com/2026/03/13/ai-super-team-mark-zuckerberg-google-gemini/): accessible and verified. Confirms Gemini licensing discussion, Avocado delay, $14.3B Alexandr Wang deal, performance between Gemini 2.5 and 3.0. (3) U.S. News/Reuters (money.usnews.com): bot-blocked (403) but Reuters wire story confirmed via American Bazaar reprint. Spokesperson quote verified verbatim.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All factual claims verified against multiple independent sources. Avocado delay March to May 2026 (NYT, Fortune, Reuters). Performance between Gemini 2.5 and 3.0 (Fortune, Reuters). Gemini 3.0 released Nov 18-20, 2025 (Google, InfoQ, TechCrunch). Weakness areas: reasoning, programming, writing (multiple outlets). Meta spokesperson quote confirmed verbatim via Reuters/American Bazaar. Gemini licensing discussion (NYT via Fortune). Capex $115B-$135B (multiple outlets). Yann LeCun departure Nov 2025 and AMI Labs (CNBC, Futurism, The Decoder). Alexandr Wang as Chief AI Officer, $14.3B Scale AI deal (CNBC, Fortune). 600 positions eliminated Oct 2025 (CNBC, TechCrunch, SiliconANGLE). Watermelon and Mango codenames (The Information, TechCrunch, The Decoder). Cloud revenue structural observation is factually accurate.
editor_notes.content_quality: Word count 576, within News range (400-1200). Well-structured with Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis sections. Effectively contextualizes the delay within Meta's broader AI strategy and competitive landscape.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference, no editorializing. The replacement analysis paragraph about cloud revenue is a genuine editorial improvement over the fabricated Jensen Huang claim.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 re-review: APPROVE. All three Round 1 issues properly resolved. (1) Fabricated Jensen Huang/Nvidia claim removed and replaced with verifiable structural observation about Meta lacking cloud revenue to offset AI infrastructure costs. (2) NYT URL fixed with www. prefix. (3) Megan Fu attribution anonymized to 'internal memo from a product manager at Meta Superintelligence Labs,' avoiding need to cite paywalled The Information source. No new issues found. Article is well-sourced, factually accurate, and editorially sound.
